name: Dynatrace API
what_it_does: Full-stack observability and APM platform with Davis AI for automated anomaly detection and root cause analysis. The v2 REST API enables agent-driven querying of metrics, traces, logs, events, and problems.
use_cases: Agent querying Davis AI problems and anomalies for automated incident response
Fetching distributed traces and spans for performance analysis automation
Metrics query and alerting policy management via API
Automated deployment event injection for change tracking
SLO monitoring and querying via the SLO API
Infrastructure topology queries to understand entity relationships
not_for: Cost-sensitive environments — Dynatrace is expensive enterprise software
Simple application monitoring where cheaper tools suffice
Workloads not deploying OneAgent (some features require the agent)
best_when: An agent operates in an enterprise environment already using Dynatrace and needs to query AI-analyzed problems, distributed traces, or full-stack topology for incident automation.
avoid_when: You don't have an existing Dynatrace investment — the cost and onboarding complexity are significant for greenfield deployments.

auth.notes: API tokens with fine-grained scope selection (per-capability permissions). OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow available for automation. Tokens created in Dynatrace Access Tokens UI with explicit scope selection. Both v1 and v2 API support tokens; v2 is strongly preferred. Environment tokens are environment-specific.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 15-day free trial with full feature access. No permanent free tier.
pricing.paid_tiers: Full-stack monitoring ~$69/month per 8GB host memory unit. Infrastructure monitoring ~$21/month per host. Digital Experience ~$11/month per 10K sessions. Enterprise contracts negotiated annually.
pricing.notes: Dynatrace pricing can escalate quickly in large environments. Full-stack monitoring requires OneAgent on every host. Most organizations pay $50K-$1M+/year for enterprise deployments.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits vary by endpoint. Metrics query: 50 requests/minute. Problems API: 50 requests/minute. Specific limits documented per endpoint in API Explorer.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: v2 API returns structured JSON errors with error.code, error.message, and error.details. API Explorer and Swagger UI are useful for discovery. Some legacy v1 endpoints have less consistent errors. Rate limit responses include Retry-After headers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET operations are inherently idempotent. Configuration PUT operations are idempotent by entity ID. Event ingestion API does not support idempotency keys — agents may create duplicate events on retry.
known_agent_gotchas: API tokens require explicit scope selection — agents need to identify and request all required scopes upfront
v1 and v2 APIs coexist with different endpoints and behaviors — always use v2 for new agent integrations
Metrics API uses DQL (Dynatrace Query Language) in newer versions — different from PromQL or SQL
OneAgent must be installed on hosts for APM data — API-only access does not provide trace data without agents
Problems API results reflect Davis AI analysis which can lag real-time by 1-3 minutes during high-load events
Entity selectors (entitySelector parameter) have a specific syntax that agents must learn to filter by type, tag, or property
Data retention varies by tier — metrics default 5 years but traces and logs may have shorter retention depending on license

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced everywhere. API tokens with fine-grained scopes are a strong security model for agents — each agent can be issued a minimally-privileged token. OAuth 2.0 available for user-facing integrations. Token rotation supported. SOC2 Type II and ISO27001 certified. FedRAMP authorized on GovCloud deployment.
